source: D59555 HAL 4.0 Risk Management Plan rev03.pdf
title: 4.0 Requirements for Risk Management Activities and their Review (Continued)
text_content: FMEA sessions require a cross-functional and technically biased team approach. FMEA analysis relies on domain knowledge of the team to identify components to consider and specific manner or way by which a failure occurs.  Analysis is conducted thru brainstorming sessions by the team.   Manner or way by which a failure occurs is highly domain (mechanical, electrical, or software) specific. Process FMEA investigates the risk in manufacturing and the supply chain that may cause material or product not meeting requirements, manufacturing operation impact or manufacturing operator impact. Contributing documents such as specifications, requirements, reports, investigations, and studies shall be reviewed by persons with recorded competence in the documents’ subjects. Where relevant, these persons shall have knowledge and experience with the device(s) in this RMP’s scope as well as familiarity with the device’s use(s) and technologies.

source: D93257 Halcyon Evolve User Assistance Plan Rev02.docx.pdf
title: 2. User Assistance Deliverables > 2.3 Quality Control of UA
text_content: Main control of content for accuracy, completeness, and quality is through reviews, internal and formal; UAD keystroking and tryout of new or changed features and procedures. Internal reviews are uncontrolled. They are intended to: Capture most changes before the formal review with longer review periods than the formal review. Ensure that all project team members have a chance to respond to the draft UA so that GPL can deliver the UA as planned. Resolve and reduce as many findings as possible before formal review. Formal reviews are controlled—findings are recorded—and require participation of the project team per RACI for Design and Development and GPL Authoring Process. For general design quality, see D95424 AL 4.0 MR2 Evolve Maintenance Release Document.

source: D65887 HAL4.0 IEC 60601-2-1 (2020) 201.10.101.1.1.5 - TERMINATION OF IRRADIATION by DOSE MONITORING SYSTEM Rev 01.docx.pdf
title: 4. IEC Compliance Checks
text_content: Compliance is checked as follows: a) b) c) e) TYPE TEST grade A – Statement regarding the means used to ensure compliance. Ref a) and c) - Grade A Type Tests – Design Statement The ability of the secondary channel, that is the BGM FPGA level, to terminate irradiation is verified between treatments as part of the dosimetry test during the mode (radiation type and energy) selection process which is called the CalCheck cycle. The CalCheck cycle includes a first check that the continuity of the cables for all ion chamber plate signals is verified. This continuity check is achieved by introducing a step change in the ion chamber power supply voltage level and then detecting the corresponding induced charges in the ion chamber collection plates. This is needed because later the dosimetry check is performed locally on the BGM-CONT only. Such a test is valid because passing the continuity test can prove that the cable connection and the connections through the ion chamber, are all intact.

source: D65887 HAL4.0 IEC 60601-2-1 (2020) 201.10.101.1.1.5 - TERMINATION OF IRRADIATION by DOSE MONITORING SYSTEM Rev 01.docx.pdf
title: 4. IEC Compliance Checks (Continued)
text_content: The proposed method involves testing of the CAL CHECK function within the BGM subsystem. A beam will be prepared in Service Mode while the Cal Check results in the Cal Check subtab are monitored. Once Cal Check is verified to complete successfully with a visual indicator at the Cal Check tab, the ion chamber’s signal cable J33 at the BGM Controller PCB will be disconnected. A second beam will be prepared while the Cal Check subtab is monitored. A successful test will be one that provokes CALCH Faults to be raised when J33 is disconnected and a beam is prepared, as well as a visual indication at the Service Mode Cal Check subtab that the Cal Check test did not pass. Results After J33 at the BGM Controller PCB was disconnected, and a beam was prepared, the following Faults were raised: 213110, 213111, 213112, 213113, 213118, 213119. These faults pertain to each of the ion chamber plate signals for plates A, B, C, D, I, and J, respectively. Conclusion IEC 60601-2-1 Edition 4.0 2020

Meanwhile, Amperemeter A show that current value is momentary increasing from 30mA to 100mA (it means out of measuring range) for about 100ms. X2, Y1, Y2, F axes get the same results. title: 4. IEC Compliance Checks (Continued)
text_content: The test passed as the expected results were achieved, showing the compliance of the Halcyon system to subclause 201.10.101.1.7a. b) SITE TEST grade B – Procedure: verify functioning of the DISPLAYS for the SPECIFIED selections. Methods The test method involved running four types of plans in Clinical Mode which utilized the following modes of operation: VMAT, IMRT, MV CBCT, and KV CBCT. The user then loaded a VMAT plan in Clinical Mode and initiated IRRADIATION. During IRRADIATION, verification of the mode of operation is performed by observing the screen at the TCP. The user then runs a plan of each type: IMRT, MV CBCT, and KV CBCT to verify the mode of operation is displayed for each plan type. A successful test will be one where the mode of operation is displayed at the TCP during IRRADIATION. title: 4.0 Requirements for Risk Management Activities and their Review (Continued)
text_content: FMEA sessions require a cross-functional and technically biased team approach. FMEA analysis relies on domain knowledge of the team to identify components to consider and specific manner or way by which a failure occurs.  Analysis is conducted thru brainstorming sessions by the team.   Manner or way by which a failure occurs is highly domain (mechanical, electrical, or software) specific. Process FMEA investigates the risk in manufacturing and the supply chain that may cause material or product not meeting requirements, manufacturing operation impact or manufacturing operator impact. Contributing documents such as specifications, requirements, reports, investigations, and studies shall be reviewed by persons with recorded competence in the documents’ subjects. Where relevant, these persons shall have knowledge and experience with the device(s) in this RMP’s scope as well as familiarity with the device’s use(s) and technologies.

title: 3.0 Definitions > 4.2 Integrity > 4.2.2 Software Integrity:
text_content: During the design and development phases of the lifecycle for the application software within Varian medical devices, the integrity of the system build is ensured by controlling the editing process and requiring regular reviews of the code. This is done to ensure that the integrity of the application software is maintained through the construction phase. W hen the software package is ready for release to the user, Varian manufacturing procedures (DND1902) are used to prevent improper or incompatible versions of the software from being distributed or installed. These procedures include measures to ensure the consistency of data, the absence of viruses or malware, and the inclusion of needed user documentation and labeling. For Varian medical devices, installation procedures and updates are handled by trained Varian Service personnel or local Service representatives.
